core_mechanism: A periodic-orbit (semiclassical closed-trajectory) mechanism in which isolated recurrent orbits of a dynamical system organize a small subset of coherent, non-ergodic modes into an equally-spaced spectral ladder whose uniform spacing is set by an orbit's fundamental frequency, standing out from an otherwise ergodic/thermalising spectrum, and persisting under weak perturbation of the underlying dynamics
brief_reason: The abstract applies a periodic-orbit framework to locate recurrent orbits across the spectrum, reconstructs the coherent (scarred) eigenstates and their equidistant towers from those orbits, and shows the orbits continue to govern the dynamics even when the exactly-solvable structure is broken by a transverse field.
